name: Classical and Conventional Phillips-Type Curves, 1948-2010

book_quotes.quote: Figures 15.7-15.9 display the scatter between inflation and (1-sigma') for the whole postwar period 1951-2010 and for sub-periods 1951-1981 and 1982-2010. For the sake of comparison, the conventional Phillips curve in terms of the unemployment rate is also depicted.
Figure 15.7 Classical and Conventional Phillip-Type Curves, 1948-2010

sigma = Growth Utilization Rate = Investment/Profit, where I = Investment = Nonresidential Private Fixed Investment, Table 5.3.5: Private Fixed Investment by Type, line 2, and Profit = Net Operating Surplus, Table 1.16. Sources and Uses of Private Enterprise Income, line 2 (taken from Handfas 2012)

primary_source.agency: Composite: BEA NIPA + BLS + Shaikh derivations
primary_source.publication: Inflation pi = % change in GDP deflator (BEA NIPA T1.1.4); growth-utilization rate sigma = I/Profit (BEA NIPA T5.3.5 line 2 / T1.16 line 2); unemployment uL from BLS (Appendix 14.2)
primary_source.table_or_series_id: BEA NIPA T1.1.4 (pGDP), T5.3.5 line 2 (Nonresidential Private Fixed Investment), T1.16 line 2 (Net Operating Surplus); BLS unemployment via Appendix 14.2
primary_source.units: Rate (decimal) for inflation, unemployment, and (1 - growth-utilization rate)

formula: Classical curve: pi vs (1 - sigma'); Conventional curve: pi vs uL. pi = first log-difference of pGDP. sigma = I/Profit per Handfas (2012). (1 - sigma') is the unutilized growth capacity.

components.label: Inflation rate (pi)
components.source: BEA NIPA T1.1.4 % change in GDP deflator

components.label: Growth-utilization rate (sigma)
components.source: BEA NIPA T5.3.5 line 2 (I) / T1.16 line 2 (Net Operating Surplus); construction per Handfas 2012

extension_candidates.subseries_label: BEA NIPA + BLS CPS modern series
extension_candidates.source: BEA NIPA + BLS via FRED (GDPDEF, PNFI, NOS proxy from corporate profits, UNRATE)
concerns: Handfas (2012) sigma uses net operating surplus; modern BEA T1.16 sectoring may have shifted.
Construction is a 4-variable composite — extension requires all four pipelines.

methodology_notes: Figure 15.7 is a Phillips-curve scatter, not a time series in the usual sense; each point is one (year, inflation, x) triple where x is either (1-sigma') for the classical curve or uL for the conventional curve.
Book's appendix description (p.896) lists the variables explicitly as 'pi, 1 - sigma, uL' for Figures 15.7-15.9.
The classical curve uses Shaikh's growth-utilization rate as the demand-pressure variable (drawn from Handfas 2012); the conventional curve uses standard BLS unemployment.
Shaikh notes a footnote: 'Using unemployment intensity ... instead of the unemployment rate does not make the conventional Phillips curve any better.' (p.707)
CD2 predecessor S083 ported with same underlying source map.
open_questions: Phase 4: confirm BEA NIPA Table 1.16 (Sources and Uses of Private Enterprise Income) is still published with this naming.
Decide whether to extend sigma using gross investment vs net operating surplus per Shaikh's footnote 1 in Appendix 15.1.

review_history.notes: CD2 S083 ported; quotes verified against PDF p.707 and Appendix 15.1 p.896
